title: Scenarios
description: Deterministic simulator scripts. Each scenario is a timed sequence of tag changes the simulator plays as if it were live data, plus the EXPECTED root cause and alarms — which turn every scenario into an automated test. This is what makes the demo repeatable and the runtime testable without hardware.

expected_situation.description: Situation type the DAG should produce (test assertion). Null for negative/degraded scenarios where no situation is expected (e.g. RS485 dropout).

expected_root_cause.description: Asset id the DAG should rank #1 (test assertion). Null when no root cause should be inferred.

expected_alarms.description: Alarm ids that should fire (test assertion).

action.description: set = step value; ramp = interpolate from→to over over_ms; fault = inject quality/comms fault.

tag.description: Tag id; must exist in tag_map.json.

fault.description: For action=fault, e.g. {type: packet_loss, rate: 0.1} or {type: stale}.
